heading: Incentive payments to States
label: 658a Incentive payments to States

L. 105–200, title II, § 201(d)112 Stat. 656July 16, 1998“(1)General review of new incentive payment system.—“(A).—In generalThe Secretary of Health and Human Services shall conduct a study of the implementation of the incentive payment system established by section 458 of the Social Security Act [], in order to identify the problems and successes of the system.42 U.S.C. 658a“(B)Reports to the congress.—“(i).—Report on variations in state performance attributable to demographic variablesNot later than , the Secretary shall submit to the Congress a report that identifies any demographic or economic variables that account for differences in the performance levels achieved by the States with respect to the performance measures used in the system, and contains the recommendations of the Secretary for such adjustments to the system as may be necessary to ensure that the relative performance of States is measured from a baseline that takes account of any such variables.October 1, 2000“(ii).—Interim reportNot later than , the Secretary shall submit to the Congress an interim report that contains the findings of the study required by subparagraph (A).March 1, 2001“(iii).—Final reportNot later than , the Secretary shall submit to the Congress a final report that contains the final findings of the study required by subparagraph (A). The report shall include any recommendations for changes in the system that the Secretary determines would improve the operation of the child support enforcement program.October 1, 2003“(2)Development of medical support incentive.—“(A).—In generalThe Secretary of Health and Human Services, in consultation with State directors of programs operated under part D of title IV of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] and representatives of children potentially eligible for medical support, shall develop a performance measure based on the effectiveness of States in establishing and enforcing medical support obligations, and shall make recommendations for the incorporation of the measure, in a revenue neutral manner, into the incentive payment system established by section 458A [now 458] of the Social Security Act [].42 U.S.C. 65142 U.S.C. 658a“(B).—ReportNot later than , the Secretary shall submit to the Congress a report that describes the performance measure and contains the recommendations required by subparagraph (A).”October 1, 1999

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: In addition to any other payment under this part, the Secretary shall, subject to subsection (f), make an incentive payment to each State for each fiscal year in an amount determined under subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The incentive payment for a State for a fiscal year is equal to the incentive payment pool for the fiscal year, multiplied by the State incentive payment share for the fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: $422,000,000 for fiscal year 2000;

children.label: ii
children.text: $429,000,000 for fiscal year 2001;

children.label: iii
children.text: $450,000,000 for fiscal year 2002;

children.label: iv
children.text: $461,000,000 for fiscal year 2003;

children.label: v
children.text: $454,000,000 for fiscal year 2004;

children.label: vi
children.text: $446,000,000 for fiscal year 2005;

children.label: vii
children.text: $458,000,000 for fiscal year 2006;

children.label: viii
children.text: $471,000,000 for fiscal year 2007;

children.label: ix
children.text: $483,000,000 for fiscal year 2008; and

children.label: x
children.text: for any succeeding fiscal year, the amount of the incentive payment pool for the fiscal year that precedes such succeeding fiscal year, multiplied by the percentage (if any) by which the CPI for such preceding fiscal year exceeds the CPI for the second preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: CPI
children.label: B CPI
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the CPI for a fiscal year is the average of the Consumer Price Index for the 12-month period ending on September 30 of the fiscal year. As used in the preceding sentence, the term “Consumer Price Index” means the last Consumer Price Index for all-urban consumers published by the Department of Labor.

children.label: A
children.text: the incentive base amount for the State for the fiscal year; divided by

children.label: B
children.text: the sum of the incentive base amounts for all of the States for the fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: The paternity establishment performance level.

children.label: B
children.text: The support order performance level.

children.label: C
children.text: The current payment performance level.

children.label: D
children.text: The arrearage payment performance level.

children.label: E
children.text: The cost-effectiveness performance level.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to the performance measures described in subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (4), the State collections base for the fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to the performance measures described in subparagraphs (D) and (E) of paragraph (4), 75 percent of the State collections base for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Data required to be complete and reliable
children.label: B Data required to be complete and reliable
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the maximum incentive base amount for a State for a fiscal year with respect to a performance measure described in paragraph (4) is zero, unless the Secretary determines, on the basis of an audit performed under , that the data which the State submitted pursuant to for the fiscal year and which is used to determine the performance level involved is complete and reliable.section 652(a)(4)(C)(i) of this titlesection 654(15)(B) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the total amount of support collected during the fiscal year under the State plan approved under this part in cases in which the support obligation involved is required to be assigned to the State pursuant to part A or E of this subchapter or subchapter XIX; and

children.label: II
children.text: the total amount of support collected during the fiscal year under the State plan approved under this part in cases in which the support obligation involved was so assigned but, at the time of collection, is not required to be so assigned; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the total amount of support collected during the fiscal year under the State plan approved under this part in all other cases.

children.heading: Determination of applicable percentages based on performance levels
children.label: 6 Determination of applicable percentages based on performance levels

children.heading: Determination of paternity establishment performance level
children.label: i Determination of paternity establishment performance level
children.text: The paternity establishment performance level for a State for a fiscal year is, at the option of the State, the IV–D paternity establishment percentage determined under or the statewide paternity establishment percentage determined under .section 652(g)(2)(A) of this titlesection 652(g)(2)(B) of this title

children.heading: Determination of applicable percentage
children.label: ii Determination of applicable percentage
children.text: The applicable percentage with respect to a State’s paternity establishment performance level is as follows:If the paternity establishment performance level is:The applicable percentage is:At least:But less than:80% 10079%80%9878%79%9677%78%9476%77%9275%76%9074%75%8873%74%8672%73%8471%72%8270%71%8069%70%7968%69%7867%68%7766%67%7665%66%7564%65%7463%64%7362%63%7261%62%7160%61%7059%60%6958%59%6857%58%6756%57%6655%56%6554%55%6453%54%6352%53%6251%52%6150%51%600%50%0.

children.heading: Establishment of child support orders
children.label: B Establishment of child support orders

children.heading: Determination of support order performance level
children.label: i Determination of support order performance level
children.text: The support order performance level for a State for a fiscal year is the percentage of the total number of cases under the State plan approved under this part in which there is a support order during the fiscal year.

children.heading: Collections on current child support due
children.label: C Collections on current child support due

children.heading: Determination of current payment performance level
children.label: i Determination of current payment performance level
children.text: The current payment performance level for a State for a fiscal year is equal to the total amount of current support collected during the fiscal year under the State plan approved under this part divided by the total amount of current support owed during the fiscal year in all cases under the State plan, expressed as a percentage.

children.heading: Collections on child support arrearages
children.label: D Collections on child support arrearages

children.heading: Determination of arrearage payment performance level
children.label: i Determination of arrearage payment performance level
children.text: The arrearage payment performance level for a State for a fiscal year is equal to the total number of cases under the State plan approved under this part in which payments of past-due child support were received during the fiscal year and part or all of the payments were distributed to the family to whom the past-due child support was owed (or, if all past-due child support owed to the family was, at the time of receipt, subject to an assignment to the State, part or all of the payments were retained by the State) divided by the total number of cases under the State plan in which there is past-due child support, expressed as a percentage.

children.heading: Determination of cost-effectiveness performance level
children.label: i Determination of cost-effectiveness performance level
children.text: The cost-effectiveness performance level for a State for a fiscal year is equal to the total amount collected during the fiscal year under the State plan approved under this part divided by the total amount expended during the fiscal year under the State plan, expressed as a ratio.

children.heading: Determination of applicable percentage
children.label: ii Determination of applicable percentage
children.text: The applicable percentage with respect to a State’s cost-effectiveness performance level is as follows:If the cost-effectiveness performance level is:The applicable percentage is:At least:But less than:5.00 1004.504.99904.004.50803.504.00703.003.50602.503.00502.002.50400.002.000.

children.heading: Treatment of interstate collections
children.label: c Treatment of interstate collections
children.text: In computing incentive payments under this section, support which is collected by a State at the request of another State shall be treated as having been collected in full by both States, and any amounts expended by a State in carrying out a special project assisted under shall be excluded.section 655(e) of this title

children.heading: Administrative provisions
children.label: d Administrative provisions
children.text: The amounts of the incentive payments to be made to the States under this section for a fiscal year shall be estimated by the Secretary at/or before the beginning of the fiscal year on the basis of the best information available. The Secretary shall make the payments for the fiscal year, on a quarterly basis (with each quarterly payment being made no later than the beginning of the quarter involved), in the amounts so estimated, reduced or increased to the extent of any overpayments or underpayments which the Secretary determines were made under this section to the States involved for prior periods and with respect to which adjustment has not already been made under this subsection. Upon the making of any estimate by the Secretary under the preceding sentence, any appropriations available for payments under this section are deemed obligated.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary governing the calculation of incentive payments under this section, including directions for excluding from the calculations certain closed cases and cases over which the States do not have jurisdiction.
to carry out the State plan approved under this part; or

for any activity (including cost-effective contracts with local agencies) approved by the Secretary, whether or not the expenditures for the activity are eligible for reimbursement under this part, which may contribute to improving the effectiveness or efficiency of the State program operated under this part.
